raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Millions of Californians are struggling with food insecurity.",
    "The hunger crisis will worsen as the federal government is gutting essential services for working families and low-income people.",
    "Assemblymember Alex Lee secured a historic investment of $36 million to revive the CalFresh Fruit and Vegetable EBT Program.",
    "The $36 million is the largest single investment in the CalFresh Fruit and Vegetable EBT Program to date.",
    "The CalFresh Fruit and Vegetable EBT Program provides up to $60/month in extra food benefits to CalFresh recipients.",
    "For every $1 of CalFresh benefits spent on fresh fruits and vegetables at participating retailers, recipients get $1 back on their EBT card.",
    "Reductions to the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) are estimated to eliminate nearly 6 to 9 billion meals annually.",
    "More families are expected to rely on food banks as food benefits decrease.",
    "The CalFresh Fruit and Vegetable EBT Program serves as a scalable and effective solution to reducing food insecurity.",
    "The pilot phase of the CalFresh Fruit and Vegetable EBT Program ran from February 2023 to April 2024 with over 90 participating retailers statewide.",
    "Participants in the CalFresh program have earned a combined total of over $10.5 million in rebates since the program's launch.",
    "Expanding access to healthy food is described as a moral obligation amid rising grocery prices and food insecurity.",
    "The $36 million investment in the CalFresh Fruit and Vegetable EBT Program will make healthy food more affordable for tens of thousands of families."
  ]
}
```
